understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to navigate the player character to the goal gem tile marked '+' on each level by using horizontal movement and click interactions to clear paths, avoiding spike hazards that cause instant failure, and managing turn limits efficiently to win the overall BP35 level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement is restricted to MOVE_LEFT and MOVE_RIGHT. After each horizontal move, the character automatically falls in the current gravity direction (e.g., DOWN) until landing on a solid tile like 'o', 'm', 'w', or hitting a spike (which ends the run). CLICK actions allow removing breakable blocks ('x') at specified coordinates, enabling new fall paths. UNDO can revert the last action. Each attempt has a turn limit (50 turns per attempt, 2 attempts per level). The structured state previews landing positions after gravity, and corridor scans indicate passages to different y-levels. The gravity direction determines fall direction: DOWN means falling toward smaller y values (upward visually), UP means falling toward larger y values (downward visually). The goal tile is always in the direction of gravity.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of the game was to navigate the player character to the goal gem tile marked '+' on each level by using horizontal movement and click interactions to clear paths, avoiding spike hazards that cause instant failure, and managing turn limits efficiently to win the overall BP35 level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement is restricted to MOVE_LEFT and MOVE_RIGHT. After each horizontal move, the character automatically falls in the current gravity direction (e.g., DOWN) until landing on a solid tile like 'o', 'm', 'w', or hitting a spike (which ends the run). CLICK actions allow removing breakable blocks ('x') at specified coordinates, enabling new fall paths. UNDO can revert the last action. Each attempt has a turn limit (50 turns per attempt, 2 attempts per level). The structured state previews landing positions after gravity, and corridor scans indicate passages to different y-levels. The gravity direction determines fall direction: DOWN means falling toward smaller y values (upward visually), UP means falling toward larger y values (downward visually). The goal tile is always in the direction of gravity.
